mappings.rationaleAI: The cellular basis of form and function represents the structural foundation of life.
The cell is the direct subject of this section as the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.
Reproductive structures and processes are fundamental aspects of cellular form and function, from gametes to zygotes.
Plant cell structure, including cell walls, chloroplasts, and vacuoles, illustrates the cellular basis of form and function.
Eukaryotic cells with nuclei and organelles exemplify the cellular basis of biological form.
